items.q: What is a key disadvantage of being a sole trader?
items.correct: Unlimited liability — the owner's personal assets are at risk if the business fails, because there is no legal separation between owner and business.
items.misconception: The main disadvantage is that sole traders can't sell any products.
items.random_wrong: Sole traders must employ at least one other person by law.
items.why: Sole traders can sell products and don't need employees. The key legal disadvantage is unlimited liability — the personal risk exposure, which is why many businesses become limited companies as they grow.

items.q: What is a brand in marketing?
items.correct: A combination of name, design, symbols and associations that distinguishes a product and creates customer perception and loyalty — more than just a logo.
items.misconception: A brand is just the logo on a product.
items.random_wrong: A brand is the company that makes a product.
items.why: Branding is the sum of perceptions, not just visual identity. Coca-Cola the brand is much more than the script logo — it is associations, taste expectations, advertising history.

items.q: What do McGregor's Theory X and Theory Y describe?
items.correct: Two contrasting management assumptions: Theory X assumes workers dislike work and need control; Theory Y assumes workers can be self-motivated and seek responsibility.
items.misconception: Theory X is about working in a factory and Theory Y is about office work.
items.random_wrong: Theory X and Theory Y are stages of business growth.
items.why: The theories are about MANAGER assumptions, not industry or growth stages. McGregor's point is that managers' beliefs about workers shape how they manage and the outcomes they get.

items.q: What does the 'S' in SMART objectives stand for?
items.why: Correct answer: Specific. “Strategic” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which 1970 essay argued the 'social responsibility of business is to increase profits'?
items.why: It's Friedman. “Drucker” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many elements are in the SMART acronym?
items.why: Exact answer: 5 elements. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
